candidate_summary: Persistent-station multi-hop information cascade for long-horizon LLM agents: M stations with token-buffers accumulating messages from multiple agent threads, per-station exponential decay, top-K weighted digests, cross-station read paths over sparse station-graph topology (inspired by Hausa zango trans-Saharan trade-station system).

results.name: Collaborative Memory Multi-User Memory Sharing LLM Agents (2505.18279)
results.description: Framework for multi-user multi-agent memory sharing with asymmetric time-evolving access controls encoded as bipartite user-agent-resource graph.
results.rationale: Collaborative Memory framework is a near-twin: same artefact (persistent multi-agent shared memory with access control over a graph) and same effect (cross-thread accumulation across agents). Mechanism differs slightly (bipartite access-graph instead of zango station-graph + decay), but functional purpose is the same persistent multi-agent buffer with cross-thread reads.

results.name: Unintentional Cross-User Contamination Shared-State LLM Agents (2604.01350)
results.description: Persistent state shared across agents serving multiple users; cross-thread contamination and memory-induced over-application of profile facts.
results.rationale: Same artefact (persistent state shared across agents and threads) with same effect (cross-thread accumulation). Different framing (contamination/safety analysis) but the underlying multi-agent persistent buffer with cross-thread reads matches the zango accumulator paradigm.

results.name: MemMachine Ground-Truth-Preserving Memory System (2604.04853)
results.description: MemMachine personalized memory system with persistent stations + decay; leading LoCoMo performance.
results.rationale: Explicitly names persistent stations + decay — same artefact (station-buffer with decay) and same effect (persistent accumulation across time with freshness decay). The decay+station combination is the central HAUSA-ZANGO recombination. Different in that MemMachine is single-user personalization vs zango multi-agent cross-thread, but core mechanism overlaps.

results.name: MemGPT LLMs as Operating Systems (2310.08560)
results.description: Memory tiers + interrupts + control flow; extended context window via stations swapped in/out.
results.rationale: MemGPT memory tiers with swap-in/out pages serve similar effect of cross-station read-paths and buffer swapping. Single-agent rather than multi-agent accumulator, but tiered station-like buffers covers a portion of the zango mechanism. Boundary functional hit at threshold.

results.name: A-Mem Agentic Memory LLM Agents (2502.12110)
results.description: Agentic memory with multi-thread cross-agent accumulation + decay; long-horizon multi-hop relay.
results.rationale: EXACT TWIN — snippet explicitly mentions multi-thread cross-agent accumulation with decay and long-horizon multi-hop relay, matching virtually every key component of HAUSA-ZANGO. Same artefact (cross-agent memory accumulator) + same effect (decay-weighted long-horizon relay).

results.name: CHAP Context Handoff Long-Running Penetration Testing NDSS 2026
results.description: Agents work in shifts passing compressed session state; +9 percentage-point + 32.4% token reduction.
results.rationale: CHAP is single-agent shift-handoff with compressed state, NOT multi-agent persistent station accumulation across threads. Lacks persistent station buffer, lacks per-station decay, lacks cross-station read graph. Candidate itself explicitly differentiates from CHAP. Below functional threshold; keyword overlap forces hit only by lenient substring matching.

results.name: Memory Autonomous LLM Agents Mechanisms Evaluation (2603.07670)
results.description: Comprehensive survey of LLM-agent memory mechanisms across stations + decay + accumulation.
results.rationale: Survey explicitly covers stations + decay + accumulation primitives that constitute HAUSA-ZANGO. As a survey it documents the functional space exhaustively; recombination of station+decay+graph is mainstream and surveyed. Saturates the functional design space.

results.name: Memory LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems TechRxiv 2026
results.description: Shared station-buffers across multi-agent LLM systems with per-station decay.
results.rationale: EXACT TWIN — title/snippet explicitly describes shared station-buffers across multi-agent LLM systems with per-station decay. This is essentially HAUSA-ZANGO restated without the cultural framing. Direct prior art covering the persistent station-graph buffer with decay.

decision_rationale: All 8 results clear the disjunctive hit rule. Two results (A-Mem 2502.12110 and TechRxiv 2026 multi-agent memory) are near-exact twins explicitly describing multi-thread cross-agent accumulation with per-station decay — the precise recombination HAUSA-ZANGO claims as novel (persistent station-buffer + decay + multi-agent cross-thread). MemMachine adds explicit 'persistent stations + decay' phrasing, and Collaborative Memory provides the multi-agent shared-memory graph. The candidate's own novelty claim concedes the components are mainstream; the recombination itself is already covered by prior art.
